abstract.text: The
protocol defines a set of optional methods that you
can use to receive map-related update messages. Because many map operations
require the
class to load data asynchronously, the map view calls
these methods to notify your application when specific operations complete. The
map view also uses these methods to request information about annotations
displayed on the map, such as the styles and interaction modes to apply to
individual annotations.
Tells the delegate that the
is missing an image. The image should be added synchronously
with
to be rendered on the current zoom level. When loading icons
asynchronously, you can load a placeholder image and replace it when your image has loaded.

methods for observing map events.
Powerful, free and open-source mapping toolkit with full control over data sources and styling.
Tells the delegate that the map view has just redrawn.
Asks the delegate whether the map view should evict cached images.
The
protocol defines a set of optional methods that you
can use to receive map-related update messages. Because many map operations
require the
class to load data asynchronously, the map view calls
these methods to notify your application when specific operations complete. The
map view also uses these methods to request information about annotations
displayed on the map, such as the styles and interaction modes to apply to
individual annotations.
An interactive, customizable map view with an interface similar to the one
provided by Apple’s MapKit.
Tells the delegate that the source changed.

Tells the delegate that one or more annotation views have been added and
positioned on the map.

The
protocol defines a set of methods that a class must
implement in order to serve as the location manager of an
. A location
manager is responsible for notifying the map view about location-related events,
such as a change in the user’s location. This protocol is similar to the
Core Location framework’s
class, but your implementation
does not need to be based on
Returns the line width in points to use when rendering the outline of a
polyline annotation.

extra information in a callout bubble.
